name: empty_null_content
description: Empty and null shapes of message content, tools, and the messages list itself. These are all characterisation cases (documenting real, verified behaviour) rather than error cases: none of them causes translate.AnthropicToOpenAI to error, but several of them cause a whole turn to silently disappear from the output, which is worth pinning down explicitly.

test_cases.description: A message whose content is the empty string "" produces NO output message at all -- the turn silently vanishes rather than becoming an empty-content message.
A message whose content is an empty block array [] behaves the same as an empty string: no output message.
A literal JSON null content value decodes without error (Go's encoding/json accepts null into the polymorphic RawMessage/string paths) and, like the empty forms, drops the turn rather than erroring.
A message object with no "content" key at all (not even null) behaves identically to an explicit null/empty content.
An explicit empty messages array combined with a system prompt still emits exactly the system message.
A request with neither "system" nor "messages" keys at all converts cleanly to zero output messages, no error.

A tool_use block whose name is the empty string is preserved as an empty (not dropped) function name -- worth pinning down since an empty function name is exactly the kind of value an upstream might reject.
An explicit empty tools array converts to zero OpenAI tools, not an error and not omitted-vs-empty confusion.
